title: Edge Analytics Container for Rolling Statistics & Threshold Logic
app: Splunk Edge Hub (custom container), gRPC SDK
spl:
index=edge-hub-data metric_name=temperature
| timechart avg(temperature) as temp_avg by host
| delta temp_avg as temp_trend
| stats avg(temp_trend) as avg_trend, stdev(temp_trend) as trend_std by host
| eval trend_anomaly=if(abs(temp_trend) > (avg_trend + (2*trend_std)), "YES", "NO")
description: Computes advanced analytics (moving averages, percentiles, trend detection) locally, reducing cloud computation burden.
value: Computes advanced analytics (moving averages, percentiles, trend detection) locally, reducing cloud computation burden.
implementation: Build custom ARM64 container with NumPy/Pandas libraries (may require multi-stage build to reduce image size). Container implements rolling window statistics (5/15/60-minute moving averages) via gRPC sensor stream. Compute percentiles, trend lines, and detect threshold crossings locally. Publish results as new metrics to MQTT (Advanced Settings → MQTT Publish) or directly to Splunk via gRPC SDK. Store raw + computed metrics locally (3M backlog) for redundancy. Configure resource limits: memory 512MB, CPU 1.5 cores.
visualization: Rolling average trend, threshold crossing frequency, anomaly detection timeline.
knownFalsePositives: Sensors offline during scheduled site visits, calibration, or replacement campaigns.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (custom container), gRPC SDK.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` computed_statistics, `index=edge-hub-logs` container_analytics_event.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Build custom ARM64 container with NumPy/Pandas libraries (may require multi-stage build to reduce image size). Container implements rolling window statistics (5/15/60-minute moving averages) via gRPC sensor stream. Compute percentiles, trend lines, and detect threshold crossings locally. Publish results as new metrics to MQTT (Advanced Settings → MQTT Publish) or directly to Splunk via gRPC SDK. Store raw + computed metrics locally (3M backlog) for redundancy. Configure resource limits: memory 5…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edge-hub-data metric_name=temperature
| timechart avg(temperature) as temp_avg by host
| delta temp_avg as temp_trend
| stats avg(temp_trend) as avg_trend, stdev(temp_trend) as trend_std by host
| eval trend_anomaly=if(abs(temp_trend) > (avg_trend + (2*trend_std)), "YES", "NO")
```

Understanding this SPL

**Edge Analytics Container for Rolling Statistics & Threshold Logic** — Computes advanced analytics (moving averages, percentiles, trend detection) locally, reducing cloud computation burden.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` computed_statistics, `index=edge-hub-logs` container_analytics_event. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (custom container), gRPC SDK. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-data.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edge-hub-data. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time with a separate series **by host** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Pipeline stage (see **Edge Analytics Container for Rolling Statistics & Threshold Logic**): delta temp_avg as temp_trend
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **trend_anomaly** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Rolling average trend, threshold crossing frequency, anomaly detection timeline.
grandmaExplanation: We watch what edge sensors report from the physical world. We help you know if something is off with what the on-site box is feeling and whether it is online before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
